title: World4RL: Diffusion World Models for Policy Refinement with Reinforcement Learning for Robotic Manipulation
authors: Zhennan Jiang; Kai Liu; Yuxin Qin; Shuai Tian; Yupeng Zheng; Mingcai Zhou; Chao Yu; Haoran Li; Dongbin Zhao
year_basis: First public appearance: arXiv:2509.19080 in Sep 2025; supplied revision v2 dated 19 Mar 2026
open_source_notes: No public code or model repository was identified in the supplied manuscript.
primary_problem: World Modeling; Policy Refinement; Reinforcement Learning
world_model_detail: A diffusion transition model predicts future RGB observations from a four-frame action-conditioned history, while a binary reward classifier scores imagined outcomes for PPO policy refinement.
prediction_target: Future RGB observations and sparse success reward
planning_method: None — PPO policy optimization inside frozen imagined rollouts rather than test-time action search
horizon_basis: The world model can autoregressively generate 50-step task trajectories, but no explicit planner optimizes a candidate action sequence at deployment.
replanning_online: Yes — the refined policy acts closed loop, but there is no explicit replanning algorithm
learning_type: Behavior Cloning; World-Model Pretraining; Proximal Policy Optimization
pretraining: Yes — policy imitation learning, diffusion dynamics pretraining, and reward-classifier training precede imagined PPO refinement
action_space: Continuous robot actions represented with two-hot encoding inside the world model
benchmark: Six Meta-World tasks and six custom real-world tasks: open/close drawer, pick bread in/out, pick apple, and press button
robot_platform: Meta-World simulated manipulators; Franka Emika Panda
main_contribution: Uses a high-fidelity diffusion world model as a frozen simulator for refining imitation-learned manipulation policies entirely through imagined PPO rollouts.
novelty: Introduces lossless two-hot continuous-action conditioning and demonstrates offline policy improvement without additional real or simulated environment interaction.
limitations: The current visual resolution and model capacity are moderate because of compute constraints, limiting imagined-rollout fidelity; policy learning can also exploit or suffer from imperfect learned dynamics.
future_work: Increase visual detail and generative capacity and develop reinforcement-learning algorithms that remain robust under imperfect world-model dynamics.
evidence_world_model: The diffusion transition model predicts subsequent observations conditioned on observation and action history, and the reward classifier evaluates imagined states.
evidence_planning: The paper explicitly contrasts its end-to-end imagined policy optimization with world-model-based test-time planning.
evidence_horizon: The evaluated tasks are individual manipulation skills with 50-step trajectories, not task-level long-horizon plans.
review_flags: Core diffusion world-model and policy-improvement paper; exclude from explicit-planner counts.
source_document: World4RL_ Diffusion World Models for Policy Refinement with Reinforcement Learning for Robotic Manipulation.txt

title: What Drives Success in Physical Planning with Joint-Embedding Predictive World Models?
authors: Basile Terver; Tsung-Yen Yang; Jean Ponce; Adrien Bardes; Yann LeCun
year_basis: First public appearance: arXiv:2512.24497 in Dec 2025; published in TMLR in May 2026
open_source_notes: Code, datasets, and checkpoints are released at the JEPA-WMs repository.
primary_problem: World Modeling; Planning; JEPA Design Study
world_model_detail: Action-conditioned JEPA world models combine a frozen visual encoder, optional proprioceptive encoder, action encoder, and an AdaLN transformer predictor trained with multistep latent rollout losses.
prediction_target: Future visual and optional proprioceptive embeddings
planning_representation: Latent goal distance with optional proprioceptive cost
horizon_basis: Planning spans 3–30 environment steps depending on the benchmark: H=3–6 with frame-skip 1 or 5; the best optimizer is CEM.
policy_type: None — actions are optimized at test time
pretraining: Yes — frozen DINOv2, DINOv3, V-JEPA, or V-JEPA-2 visual encoders are studied
goal_representation: Goal image encoded in latent space
benchmark: PointMaze; Wall; Push-T; Meta-World; RoboCasa; DROID
robot_platform: Simulated navigation/manipulation agents; RoboCasa Franka; offline DROID real-robot data
main_contribution: Systematically identifies the architecture, training, representation, and optimizer choices that drive successful planning with pretrained-encoder JEPA world models.
novelty: Provides a unified JEPA-WM recipe and finds that proprioception, multistep rollout training, task-appropriate context, AdaLN conditioning, and CEM materially improve planning.
limitations: Deterministic MSE predictors model the conditional mean of multimodal futures, reward-free goal-distance planning cannot directly optimize cumulative reward, and real-data experiments reveal camera and action-calibration errors.
future_work: Develop stochastic JEPA dynamics, such as latent-variable or latent-diffusion predictors, and integrate reward models where cumulative reward is required.
evidence_world_model: The predictor autoregressively forecasts future embeddings from encoded observations, proprioception, and candidate actions.
evidence_planning: CEM, NeverGrad, Adam, and gradient descent are compared for optimizing action sequences through latent model rollouts; CEM is recommended.
evidence_horizon: The largest normalized horizon is six actions with frame-skip five, equivalent to 30 environment steps, so planning_horizon is Medium.
review_flags: Core JEPA planning and design-study paper. Real-world robot data are used, but no physical robot deployment is reported.
source_document: What Drives Successs in Physical Planning with Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture.txt

title: WEAVER, Better, Faster, Longer: An Effective World Model for Robotic Manipulation
authors: Arnav Kumar Jain; Yilin Wu; Jesse Farebrother; Gokul Swamy; Andrea Bajcsy
year_basis: First public appearance: arXiv:2606.13672 in Jun 2026; supplied revision v2 dated 16 Jun 2026
open_source_notes: Code, models, and videos are released through the WEAVER project page.
primary_problem: World Modeling; Policy Evaluation; Policy Improvement; Test-Time Planning
world_model_detail: A multi-view latent flow-matching world model combines diffusion forcing, pretrained encoders and decoders, sparse long-term memory, recent history, and latent reward and critic heads.
prediction_target: Future multi-view visual and proprioceptive latents, rewards, and values
video_prediction: Yes — predicted latents can be decoded into future multi-view observations
planning_representation: Latent predicted advantage from reward and critic heads
horizon_basis: Test-time steering samples four action chunks and imagines h=12 steps; the authors characterize this as short-horizon single-chunk planning constrained by latency.
learning_type: Flow-Matching World-Model Learning; Reward and Value Learning; Synthetic-Data Policy Fine-Tuning
policy_type: π0.5 Vision-Language-Action Policy with Best-of-N Steering or Synthetic-Data Fine-Tuning
pretraining: Yes — pretrained on DROID with pretrained encoder/decoder components and the π0.5 foundation policy
observation_modality: Language; wrist and external RGB views; proprioception; temporal memory
goal_representation: Natural-language instruction scored by learned reward and value heads
benchmark: DROID validation and five real tasks: Stack Bowls, PnP Bag, PnP Marker, PnP Towel, and Pour Beans
robot_platform: Franka Emika Panda with external Zed 2i and wrist Zed Mini cameras
main_contribution: Builds a robot world model that simultaneously supports accurate long-horizon evaluation, synthetic-data policy improvement, and low-latency test-time planning.
novelty: Combines latent future prediction, flow matching, diffusion forcing, multi-view memory, pretrained decoding, and latent reward/value verification in one efficient system.
limitations: Partial observability, deformable and dynamic contact interactions, limited embodiment and data coverage, noisy reward supervision, and generation latency still restrict planning to one action chunk.
future_work: Use richer sensing such as depth and touch, add physics priors, improve memory under occlusion, accelerate generation for longer lookahead, diversify embodiments, and improve reward supervision.
evidence_world_model: WEAVER predicts action-conditioned future latent sequences, decodable observations, and instruction-conditioned rewards and values.
evidence_planning: At test time, it samples four action chunks, imagines each outcome, scores predicted advantage, and executes the best candidate.
evidence_horizon: The world model evaluates rollouts lasting up to 40 seconds, while explicit test-time planning uses a single 12-step action chunk.
review_flags: Core long-horizon world model with explicit test-time planning; task horizon is Long while numeric planning_horizon is Medium.
source_document: WEAVER, Better, Faster, Longer_ An Effective World Model for Robotic Manipulation.txt

title: VoxPoser: Composable 3D Value Maps for Robotic Manipulation with Language Models
authors: Wenlong Huang; Chen Wang; Ruohan Zhang; Yunzhu Li; Jiajun Wu; Li Fei-Fei
year_basis: First public appearance: arXiv:2307.05973 in Jul 2023; published at CoRL 2023
open_source_notes: Code and videos are publicly released through the VoxPoser project.
primary_problem: Planning; 3D Value Maps; Language Grounding; Optional Dynamics Learning
human_robot_interaction: Yes — supports free-form instructions and corrective language feedback
world_model_detail: Most zero-shot tasks use known or heuristic models, while contact-rich tasks learn a task-specific MLP next-state dynamics model from online transitions guided by an LLM-generated trajectory prior.
prediction_target: Next task-relevant object or scene state
planning_method: Hierarchical Language Planner; MPC; Zeroth-Order Trajectory Optimization
planning_representation: Composable 3D voxel affordance, avoidance, rotation, velocity, and gripper maps
horizon_basis: An LLM decomposes arbitrarily long instructions into task phases, while the low-level controller replans dense 6-DoF trajectories at 5 Hz.
learning_type: Zero-Shot Foundation-Model Prompting; Optional Online Dynamics Learning
pretraining: Yes — pretrained LLM and VLM components
observation_modality: Natural language; dual RGB-D cameras; 3D voxel and point-cloud representations
goal_representation: Free-form language converted into 3D value-map objectives
action_space: Continuous 6-DoF end-effector waypoints with gripper and velocity parameters
benchmark: Five real-world everyday tasks; 13 simulated task families with 2,766 instructions; contact-rich door, window, and refrigerator tasks
main_contribution: Uses LLM-generated code and VLM perception to compose dense 3D value maps that directly serve as objectives for closed-loop robot trajectory optimization.
novelty: Grounds open-world language affordances and constraints spatially instead of selecting a fixed skill primitive, while using synthesized trajectories as efficient priors for dynamics learning.
limitations: Depends on external perception modules and manual prompt engineering, lacks a general-purpose contact dynamics model, and optimizes only end-effector rather than whole-arm trajectories.
future_work: Use multimodal LLMs for direct grounding, reduce prompt engineering through alignment methods, learn more general dynamics, and develop stronger whole-arm trajectory optimizers.
evidence_world_model: For contact-rich tasks, an action-conditioned MLP predicts the next observation and is learned online from transitions collected around a zero-shot trajectory prior.
evidence_planning: LLMs decompose instructions, compose value-map costs, and a closed-loop MPC optimizer repeatedly samples and scores trajectories.
evidence_horizon: The framework supports task-level decomposition and multi-step programs, while each low-level trajectory is replanned continuously.
review_flags: World-model use is optional and task-specific: most zero-shot experiments use known or heuristic dynamics, but the paper includes an evaluated learned predictive model.
source_document: VoxPoser_ Composable 3D Value Maps for Robotic Manipulation with Language Models.txt

title: VLMPC: Vision-Language Model Predictive Control for Robotic Manipulation
authors: Wentao Zhao; Jiaming Chen; Ziyu Meng; Donghui Mao; Ran Song; Wei Zhang
year_basis: Published at Robotics: Science and Systems 2024
open_source_notes: Code is publicly released at the VLMPC repository.
task_domain: Language-Guided Manipulation; Vision-Based Model Predictive Control
language_conditioned: Yes — also accepts a goal image
world_model_detail: DMVFN-Act is a lightweight action-conditioned video predictor that autoregressively generates future RGB frames for VLM-guided candidate action sequences.
prediction_target: Future RGB frames under candidate action sequences
planning_representation: Predicted videos scored by pixel-level and VLM knowledge-level costs
horizon_basis: Each receding-horizon iteration predicts a finite T-action sequence, executes only the first action, and repeats; the main paper does not report a normalized numeric T.
hierarchical_planning: Yes — VLM-derived subgoals and interference objects guide a lower-level video MPC loop
learning_type: Supervised Action-Conditioned Video Prediction; Foundation-Model Prompting
pretraining: Yes — GPT-4V and Qwen-VL plus DMVFN-Act pretraining on three Open X-Embodiment subsets
observation_modality: Monocular RGB; goal image or natural-language instruction; recent executed actions
goal_representation: Goal image or natural-language instruction decomposed into visual subgoals
action_space: Continuous 7D translation, rotation, and gripper actions
benchmark: RoboDesk seven-task VP2 benchmark; Language Table move-to-area across 50 environments; four real-world tasks
robot_platform: Simulated Franka Panda; real UR5 robot
main_contribution: Integrates VLM-guided action sampling and semantic video scoring with action-conditioned visual MPC for open-set robotic manipulation.
novelty: Uses VLMs both to bias candidate trajectories toward semantic subgoals and to evaluate predicted futures beyond pixel similarity.
limitations: Predicted videos can be inconsistent with their conditioning actions, VLM calls add substantial computation, and sparse semantic evaluation can fail in tightly constrained motion spaces.
future_work: Develop more reliable action-conditioned video prediction and a more efficient method for integrating large VLMs into every MPC step.
evidence_world_model: DMVFN-Act autoregressively predicts future observations frame by frame from past images and candidate actions.
evidence_planning: VLMPC samples candidate action sequences, predicts their videos, scores them with hierarchical costs, and executes the first action of the best sequence.
evidence_horizon: Real tasks such as putting a banana in a bowl and wiping water require multiple subgoals and can run for several minutes, while each explicit MPC rollout is short.
review_flags: Core pixel-space world-model planner. Task horizon is Long, but numeric MPC horizon is classified Short because T is not reported in the main paper.
source_document: VLMPC_ Vision-Language Model Predictive Control for Robotic Manipulation.txt
